name: Feedly
description: RSS reader and news aggregator with AI-powered filtering, boards, and integrations with productivity tools.

name: Miniflux
description: Minimalist, self-hosted RSS reader written in Go. Fast, opinionated, and privacy-respecting with a clean interface.

name: NetNewsWire
description: Free, open-source RSS reader for Apple platforms. Fast, native, and syncs via iCloud, Feedbin, or Feedly.

name: Inoreader
description: Powerful RSS reader with rules, filters, and monitoring. Tracks newsletters, social feeds, and web pages too.

name: FreshRSS
description: Self-hosted RSS aggregator supporting multiple users. Lightweight, extensible, and compatible with Google Reader API.

name: NewsBlur
description: RSS reader with intelligence training. Teaches it what you like and it highlights or hides stories accordingly.

name: Readwise Reader
description: Read-it-later app with built-in RSS, newsletter support, and highlights that sync to your note-taking tools.

name: Mastodon
description: Decentralized social network using the ActivityPub protocol. Self-hosted instances, no ads, and no algorithm.

name: Bluesky
description: Decentralized social network built on the AT Protocol. Custom feeds, domain-based identity, and open ecosystem.

name: Buffer
description: Social media scheduling and publishing tool. Plan, queue, and analyze posts across multiple platforms.

name: Ivory
description: Premium Mastodon client for Apple platforms by Tapbots. Beautiful timeline, full-text search, and multiple accounts.

name: Ice Cubes
description: Open-source Mastodon client for iOS and macOS built with SwiftUI. Fast, customizable timeline and multi-account.

name: Elk
description: Web-based Mastodon client with a clean, modern interface. Nuxt-powered, keyboard-friendly, and mobile-responsive.

name: Megalodon
description: Fork of the official Mastodon Android app with extra features like local-only posts, quote posts, and timeline filters.

name: Google Meet
description: Google's video conferencing platform. Integrated with Google Calendar and Workspace, live captions, and recording.

name: Jitsi Meet
description: Free, open-source video conferencing. No account required, self-hostable, end-to-end encrypted, and browser-based.

name: Webex
description: Cisco's enterprise video conferencing platform. HD video, screen sharing, whiteboarding, and hybrid event hosting.

name: LiveKit
description: Open-source platform for building real-time video, audio, and data experiences. WebRTC SFU with SDKs for every platform.

name: Whereby
description: Browser-based video meetings with no downloads required. Permanent room links, embeddable, and simple to join.

name: Tiny Tiny RSS
description: Self-hosted RSS reader with a web interface. Supports plugins, filters, labels, and keyboard shortcuts.

name: Feedbin
description: Clean, fast RSS reader with email newsletter support. Syncs with many third-party apps and offers a hosted experience.

name: Reeder
description: Beautiful native RSS reader for Apple platforms. Supports Feedbin, iCloud, Feedly, and local feeds with a minimal design.

name: Tusky
description: Lightweight, open-source Mastodon client for Android. Material Design, multi-account support, and drafts.

name: Hootsuite
description: Social media management platform for scheduling posts, monitoring mentions, and analytics across multiple networks.

name: TweetDeck
description: Multi-column X/Twitter client for power users. Monitor multiple timelines, searches, and lists simultaneously.

name: GoTo Meeting
description: Online meeting and video conferencing tool by GoTo. Screen sharing, recording, and meeting transcription.

name: Daily
description: Developer-first video and audio API platform. Embed real-time communication into apps with WebRTC and SDKs.

name: Element Call
description: Decentralized video conferencing built on Matrix. End-to-end encrypted, self-hostable, and browser-based.
